zh.lifeSummary: 當Alice de Bois於大約1310年出生在英國時，她的父親Sir Robert de Bois III3歲，母親Joanne de Etchingham22歲。她於1334年與John Howard在英國英格蘭波克夏瓦林福德結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和6個女兒的父母。她去世於1374年在英國諾福爾可易亞斯特 文其享年65歲，埋葬在英國諾福爾可易亞斯特 文其。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Alice de Bois于大约1310年出生在英国时，她的父亲Sir Robert de Bois III3岁，母亲Joanne de Etchingham22岁。她于1334年与John Howard在英国英格兰波克夏瓦林福德结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和6个女儿的父母。她去世于1374年在英国諾福爾可易亞斯特 文其享年65岁，埋葬在英国諾福爾可易亞斯特 文其。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Bois
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Alcide, Antoine, Donat, Marcel, Ovide, Ovila, Raoul, Serge.

French: topographic name for someone living or working in a wood, from (Old) French bois ‘wood’ (from Late Latin boscum ‘bush, shrub, undergrowth’, of ancient Germanic origin), or a habitational name from (Le) Bois, the name of several places in various parts of France. This surname is also found in Haiti. Compare Debois and the most frequent French form Dubois . Compare also Boie , Boies , and Boyce .
English: variant of   Boyce   and   Boyes  .

variants.name: Dubois
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Armand, Normand, Jacques, Pierre, Fernand, Marcel, Emile, Lucien, Michel, Alain, Luc.

French, Walloon, and English (of French Huguenot origin): topographic name, with fused preposition and definite article du ‘from the’, for someone living or working in a wood (see Bois ), or a habitational name for someone from any of several places in various parts of France and in Belgium (Wallonia) named Le Bois. This is the most frequent surname in Wallonia. It is also found in the Flemish part of Belgium, the Netherlands, Germany, and Haiti. In both England and North America it is sometimes translated into English as Wood . Compare Deboe , Debois , Deboy , Duboise , and Dubose .

History: This surname is listed (in the form Du Bois) in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of America. One of the several Huguenot ancestors bearing this name was Chrétien Du Bois from Flanders, whose son, Louis Du Bois (born 1626 in Lille, France, died 1696 in Kingston, NY), was among the Huguenots who founded the town of New Paltz, NY 1675–78.

variants.name: Boisvert
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Marcel, Armand, Andre, Donat, Lucien, Jacques, Gaston, Normand, Pierre, Gilles, Herve, Adrien.
French:
topographic name for someone who lived in a dense forest or perhaps a copse of evergreens, from Old French bois ‘wood’ (see Bois ) + vert, verd ‘green’.

variants.name: Boisclair
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Normand, Marcel, Aldege, Alphonse, Arsene, Edmour, Emile, Leopaul, Prudent, Rejean, Remi, Yves.

French: topographic name from Old French bois ‘wood’ (see Bois ) + clair ‘clear, light’, or a habitational name from (Le) Bois Clair, the name of several places in various parts of France. This surname is virtually non-existing in France.

variants.name: Boies
meanings: Altered form of French   Bois  .

variants.name: Baskerville
meanings: English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Boscherville in Eure, France, named with Old Anglo-Norman French boschet ‘copse, thicket’ (a diminutive of Bois ) + ville ‘settlement, town’.

variants.name: Debois
meanings: Altered form of French   Dubois  , a cognate of 1 above.
French: topographic name, with fused preposition de ‘from’, for someone living or working in a wood (see Bois ).

nameMeanings.name: Alice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a variant of Adelaide , representing an Old French spelling of a reduced form of Germanic Adalheidis. Alice and Adelaide were already regarded as distinct names in English during the medieval period. Alice enjoyed a surge of popularity in the 19th century and periods of favour ever since. It was the name of the central character of Lewis Carroll's Alice's Adventures in Wonderland ( 1865 ) and Through the Looking Glass ( 1872 ), who was based on his child friend Alice Liddell , daughter of the Dean of Christ Church, Oxford.

variants.name: Alys
meanings: Variant spelling of   Alice .

variants.name: Alis
meanings: equivalent of Alice .

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Alice de Bois was born about 1310, in England, her father, Sir Robert de Bois III, was 3 and her mother, Joanne de Etchingham, was 22. She married John Howard in 1334, in Wallingford, Berkshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 6 daughters. She died in 1374, in East Winch, Norfolk, England, at the age of 65, and was buried in East Winch, Norfolk, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Alice de Bois est née environ 1310, à Angleterre, son père, Sir Robert de Bois III, avait 3 ans et sa mère, Joanne d’Etchingham, avait 22 ans. Elle a épousé John Howard en 1334, à Wallingford, Berkshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 6 filles. Elle est décédée en 1374, à East Winch, Norfolk, Angleterre, à l’âge de 65 ans, et a été enterrée à East Winch, Norfolk, Angleterre.

Sie hat 1334 in Wallingford, Berkshire, England, United Kingdom John Howard geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 6 Töchter. Sie starb 1374 im Alter von 65 Jahren in East Winch, Norfolk, England und wurde in East Winch, Norfolk, England begraben.

ko.lifeSummary: Alice de Bois(이)가 대략 1310년 영국에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Robert de Bois III(은)는 3세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Joanne de Etchingham(은)는 22세였다. 그녀는 1334년 영국 잉글랜드 버크실르 월링퍼드에서 John Howard(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1374년 영국 노르폴크 이스트 윈치에서 65세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 노르폴크 이스트 윈치에 묻혔다.

Ela casou-se com John Howard em 1334, em Wallingford, Berkshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 6 filhas. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Alice de Bois родилась около 1310 г. в местности Англия, её отцу по имени Sir Robert de Bois III было 3 года, а матери, которую звали Joanne de Etchingham, 22. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Howard в 1334 г. в местности валлингфорд, биркшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 6 дочерей. Она умерла в 1374 г. в местности эаст винч, норфолк, Англия в возрасте 65 лет и была похоронена в местности эаст винч, норфолк, Англия.
